commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7b36dfcf3ee9e3c9bbcdd5bf7a25c73c6565d9eb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Telegram provider proof metadata, a post-send outbound validation hook, Telegram topic proof validation, and regression tests for cron Telegram topic delivery.

PR surface: Source +412, Tests +654. Total +1066 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection shows the PR exports a new plugin-facing validation hook and invokes it after send results are available. The original live Telegram topic behavior is not reproduced because the PR has no live Telegram artifact.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer API/compatibility judgment and contributor live Telegram proof; the remaining blockers are not a safe narrow automation repair.

target.title: fix(agents): keep lightweight cron prompts out of no-op compaction
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96658.

Lightweight isolated cron runs on very small context windows can reserve too much of the model window before the first prompt is sent. In the reported 4,096-token fallback-model case, current `main` leaves only 2,048 prompt tokens for a 3,544-token lightweight cron prompt, so the precheck routes to `compact_only` even though the lightweight bootstrap has no useful history to compact.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the shared small-context prompt floor conservative: `MIN_PROMPT_BUDGET_RATIO` remains `0.5` for full-context callers, settings reserve capping, CLI compaction, mid-turn guard paths, prompt-image runs, tool-enabled runs, and lightweight shared-history runs.

The higher 87.5% prompt share is scoped only to history-free lightweight pre-prompt admission with no prompt images and no active tool schemas. This intentionally does not solve provider-visible tool-schema budgeting; tool-enabled lightweight runs stay on the shared floor instead of trying to fit near the model boundary.

No SDK shape, config, docs, workflow, provider transport, or global budget policy is changed.

## User Impact

History-free lightweight cron jobs on small-context fallback models can proceed when the rendered prompt itself fits the model window, instead of entering no-op compact-only recovery with nothing useful to compact.

Tool-enabled runs, prompt-image runs, shared-history lightweight runs, and non-lightweight/full-context paths keep conservative behavior. Provider/tool-heavy budgeting is left out of scope for this PR.

## Evidence

Current-head behavior proof for the reported 4,096-token / 20,000-reserve boundary:

```text
history-free lightweight, no prompt images, no active tools: route=fits, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3584, effectiveReserveTokens=512
history-free lightweight with prompt image: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
history-free lightweight with active tools: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
lightweight with shared history: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
full-context mode: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
```

Focused validation on head `ac34cf36327`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts
# PASS: 1 file, 21 tests

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# PASS

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts
# PASS

git diff --check
# PASS
```

Current PR surface after narrowing:

```text
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts                  +3/-0
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts    +20/-1
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts +77/-0
Total: +100/-1 across 3 files
```

Review comments addressed:

- Scope reduction: reverted the provider-visible OpenAI Responses/Completions tool-budgeting layer and its follow-up lint commit.
- Codex / ClawSweeper image follow-up: prompt-image lightweight runs stay on the shared floor.
- ClawSweeper tool follow-up, narrowed form: active-tool lightweight runs stay on the shared floor; this PR no longer claims provider/tool-heavy fits.

Autoreview: attempted one local draft-PR review round earlier, but the helper failed before review due local Codex config parsing (`service_tier = priority`; helper expected `fast` or `flex`). No autoreview findings were produced.

Agent transcript: a matching local session was found, but no transcript is included because adding sanitized transcript logs requires explicit approval.
